data.title: Cipher Mining stock up amid convertible senior notes offering announcement
data.description:
Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) stock was trading higher as the data center developer announced a $800M 0.00% convertible senior notes offering to institutional buyers.

Shares were +3.75% Thursday pre-market to $14.67.

The senior, unsecured obligations mature on October 1, 2031, will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount will not accrete.

Also, the initial purchasers are expected to be granted a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $120M of the notes.

The final terms of the notes, including the initial conversion rate and certain other terms, will be determined at the time of pricing.

The conversions are set to be settled by paying or delivering cash, shares of common stock, or a combination of cash and shares of common stock, at Cipher's election. 

Redemption price for the notes will be equal to the principal amount, plus accrued and unpaid special interest and additional interest, if any.

Net proceeds from the offering are intended to be used to fund the cost of entering into related capped call transactions, finance a portion of the data center construction at Barber Lake, accelerate the build-out of its high-performance computing strategy across the 2.4 GW pipeline, continue to expand the pipeline of development sites, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Stock index futures muted ahead of a slew of macroeconomic releases, Fed speak
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted on Thursday, as investors awaited a slew of macroeconomic releases and Fed speak slated for the day.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.14%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.61%.

Wall Street's major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, marking a second day of declines, as struggling U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

“Risk assets have struggled to find their footing over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 posting another modest decline after the AI-driven selloff on Tuesday. Overnight futures have recouped around half those losses, but it does seem that we've paused for breath in recent days. There hasn’t been a major catalyst driving this, but decent housing data and stronger oil prices yesterday led to some doubts about the prospect of rapid rate cuts from the Federal Reserve,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

There is a barrage of economic releases on Thursday. 

The August durable goods orders will come in before the opening bell. Total durable goods orders are seen showing a decrease of 0.5% on the month.

GDP will land at the same time. The final revision for Q2 growth is expected to show no change from 3.3% in the last report.

“The U.S. releases revised second-quarter GDP data. The market consensus is for no change. U.S. growth has been distorted this year—export and inventory data swung wildly between the first and second quarters. Unfortunately, the fiction of annualization assumes that what happens in a quarter, however unique, is continually repeated. This exaggerates first-quarter weakness and second-quarter strength,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The initial jobless claims and international trade in goods (advance) will also come in at the same time. Claims are seen at 238K in the latest week and the consensus sees the goods trade gap at $94.4 billion in August versus $103.9 billion in July.

“U.S. initial and continuing jobless claims data will get attention, as the Fed prioritizes weak labor markets over higher inflation,” Donovan said. 

During market hours, the August existing home sales report will land. Sales are expected to stay soft at a 3.95 million rate in August versus 4.01 million in July.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day. 

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, are all slated to take the stage in different events.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: CarMax misses Q2 estimates; plans $150M in cost cuts over 18 months
Danish airport briefly shut after drone sightings for the second time this week
data.description:
Denmark's Aalborg airport was closed for hours because of unauthorized drones in its airspace, police said on Thursday, just days after the country's main Copenhagen airport was shut due to drone sightings.

Drone were spotted overnight near Aalborg airport as well as three smaller airports in Esbjerg, Sønderborg and Skrydstrup. To note, the Aalborg airport is used for commercial and military flights, while Skrydstrup has a military base that's home to Denmark's F-16 and F-35 jets.

"There can be no doubt that everything points to this being the work of a professional actor when we are talking about such a systematic operation in so many locations at virtually the same time," Danish Defense Minister Troels Lund Poulsen said. "This is what I would define as a hybrid attack using different types of drones." 

Danish police said it's too early to determine who was operating the drones and what was their goal.

On Monday, two or three large drones were seen near Copenhagen airport, prompting its shutdown for nearly four hours. Authorities in Norway also closed the Oslo airport for three hours that evening after a drone was spotted.

Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen had called the drone activity near Copenhagen the "most serious attack" on Danish critical infrastructure, adding that it fit in with other recent drone attacks, airspace violations, and hacker attacks on European airports.

data.title: Blackberry beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q3 and updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Blackberry press release (NYSE:BB): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $129.6M (+2.7% Y/Y) beats by $7.57M.

  • Total company GAAP and adjusted gross margin increased by 4 percentage points year-over-year to 75%.

  • QNX revenue beat guidance and achieved 15% year-over-year growth to $63.1 million; QNX segment adjusted gross margin were flat year-over-year at 83%

  • QNX adjusted EBITDA beat previously-provided guidance at $20.5 million, or 32% of revenue.

  • Total company adjusted EBITDA exceeded previously-provided guidance at $25.9 million, or 20% of revenue.

  • Operating cash flow for the second quarter beat expectations at positive $3.4 million.

  • Total cash, cash equivalents, short-term and long-term investments therefore decreased by $18.4 million sequentially to $363.5 million.

data.title: Cipher Mining jumps as Google takes 5% stake via Fluidstack AI hosting deal
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) is taking an approximately 5.4% pro forma equity stake in Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) as part of a 10-year high-performance computing hosting agreement between Cipher and AI cloud platform Fluidstack.

Shares of Cipher are up around 11% ahead of market open on Thursday. Google will receive warrants to acquire around 24 million shares of Cipher common stock, equating to a 5.4% pro forma equity ownership stake under the deal.

Google will also backstop $1.4B of Fluidstack’s lease obligations to support project-related debt financing.

Under the deal, Cipher will provide 168 MW of critical IT load at its Barber Lake site in Colorado City, Texas, to Fluidstack, with delivery expected by September 2026.

The agreement is expected to generate $3B in revenue over 10 years for Cipher. Two five-year extension options could increase total contract revenue to $7B.

The agreement positions Cipher, with its growing 2.4 GW data center pipeline, as a major developer of AI and HPC infrastructure.

“This transformative transaction reinforces our HPC momentum as we continue to attract attention for our large and growing pipeline of sites,” said Cipher CEO Tyler Page.

Fluidstack co-founder César Maklary added, “Together with Cipher Mining, we’re committed to accelerating the development of critical infrastructure on which frontier AI companies depend.”

The Barber Lake site has potential for up to 500 MW of capacity expansion on 587 acres, providing ample space for next-generation compute operations. Cipher plans to retain full project ownership while accessing capital markets as needed to fund construction.

data.title: Aldi makes another bold move in the U.S. with a major rebrand of its private labels
data.description:
Aldi is making another aggressive move in the U.S. grocery sector by rebranding its private label portfolio and launching its first-ever namesake brand. The rebrand will take place over the next few years. The Germany-founded company said the move is a direct response to customer feedback.

"The new look and feel of our products is the next step in our journey to modernize our simpler, quicker shopping experience. Now, it’s easier than ever for shoppers to instantly spot the value and quality only ALDI can deliver," said Atty McGrath, who began her role as CEO of Aldi on September 1. "After nearly 50 years of setting the standard in private label, our updated packaging will give shoppers yet another reason to reach for our products first," she added.

More than 90% of Aldi products are private label, and while the quality of items shoppers know won't change, they will get a fresh new look. Several brands will be replaced with the ALDI name, while iconic brands like Clancy’s, Simply Nature, and Specially Selected will remain on shelves with modernized branding and the bold “an ALDI Original” endorsement. Other items like “Red Bag Chicken” will adopt shopper-given nicknames.

Earlier this month, Aldi confirmed to Grocery Dive that it plans to add another 200 stores in the U.S. as it continues to grow faster than peers and take advantage of the push by consumers to find value,

Notably, Aldi's core strategy focuses on offering low prices through private labels, efficiency, and cost controls. The narrow focus has helped to boost Aldi's global brand recognition in the era of inflation-weary consumers. Looking ahead, Aldi plans to set up its largest New York City store next summer near Times Square in a high-profile opening. Aldi has a U.S. headquarters in Batavia, Illinois, outside Chicago.

Aldi has a dual corporate structure, split in 1961 into the separate family-owned Aldi South and Aldi Nord businesses, which operate in different geographic regions. The company has a U.S. headquarters located in Batavia, Illinois.

Aldi competes in the U.S. to varying degrees with Kroger (NYSE:KR), Safeway (NYSE:ACI), Albertsons (NYSE:ACI), Grocery Outlet (GO), Publix, Walmart (WMT), Costco (COST), Target (TGT), Trader Joe's, Dollar General (DG), Dollar Tree (DLTR), and Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM). In Europe, Aldi competes with Lidl, Carrefour (OTCPK:CRRFY) (OTCPK:CRERF), Tesco (OTCPK:TSCDF) (OTCPK:TSCDY), Asda, and Sainsbury (OTCQX:JSNSF) (OTCQX:JSAIY).

data.title: CarMax GAAP EPS of $0.64 misses by $0.39, revenue of $6.59B misses by $430M
data.description:
• CarMax press release (NYSE:KMX): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.64 misses by $0.39.
  • Revenue of $6.59B (-6.0% Y/Y) misses by $430M.
  • Retail used unit sales decreased 5.4% and comparable store used unit sales decreased 6.3%; wholesale units decreased 2.2%.
  • Solid unit margins with gross profit per retail used unit of $2,216, gross profit per wholesale unit of $993, and Extended Protection Plans margin per retail unit of $576, all in line with the prior year’s second quarter.
  • Bought 293,000 vehicles from consumers and dealers, a decrease of 2.4%.
  • As of August 31, 2025, the allowance for loan losses of $507.3 million was 3.02% of auto loans held for investment, up from 2.76% as of May 31, 2025.

data.title: Accenture beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Accenture press release (NYSE:ACN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.03 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $17.6B (+7.3% Y/Y) beats by $240M.
  • New bookings of $21.3 billion for the quarter and $80.6 billion for the year.
  • Generative AI new bookings of $1.8 billion for the quarter and $5.9 billion for the year.
  • Adjusted1 operating margin of 15.1%, an increase of 10 basis points.
  • Free cash flow of $3.8 billion for the quarter and $10.9 billion for the year

    • Fiscal Year 2026 Business Outlook Highlights

    • Company expects full-year revenue growth of 2% to 5% in local currency vs. estimated growth of 5.28% Y/Y; excluding a 1% to 1.5% impact from its U.S. federal business, company expects revenue growth of 3% to 6% in local currency
    • Expects full-year GAAP diluted EPS of $13.19 to $13.57, a 9% to 12% increase; expects full-year adjusted EPS of $13.52 to $13.90 vs. consensus of $13.79, a 5% to 8% increase.
    • The company expects to return at least $9.3 billion in cash to shareholders in fiscal year 2026.
    • Q1 Revenue consensus of $18.52B

data.title: Archer Aviation sets an altitude record as its test flights continue
data.description: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE:ACHR) continues to work towards its goal to develop the key enabling technologies and aircraft necessary to power the future of aviation. The company announced earlier in the week that its Midnight aircraft successfully completed its highest altitude flight to date, reaching altitudes of 7,000 feet.The flight took off from Archer’s test facility in Salinas, California. During the flight, Midnight’s test pilot conducted a steady climb after takeoff, leveled off at altitude, then continued to fly for 45 miles in total at speeds exceeding 120 mph. The achievement follows last month’s record-setting flight in which Midnight covered approximately 55 miles in 31 minutes at speeds exceeding 126 mph.The company said the flight marked one in a series of upcoming aircraft performance test flights designed to test and showcase Midnight’s operational range. While eVTOL aircraft are primarily intended to operate in and around cities at altitudes between 1,500 and 4,000 feet. Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) noted that the operational capability to operate at higher altitudes provides flexibility for managing air traffic in high-density environments and allows the aircraft to be able to operate in certain cities at elevation."Our test pilots will continue to safely and methodically test Midnight’s speed, duration, and mission profiles to prepare for early commercial operations," stated CEO Adam Goldstein.
